export_view.value:
This article provides an overview of how to effectively track and process warranty claims for customer devices using our point-of-sale system. It covers setup, claim processing, reconciliation, and reporting.

Additional Resources

  • Invoicing: Creating and Managing Invoices

  • Setting Up Billing Agents

By following this guide, you can streamline warranty claims processing, ensuring accurate tracking and reconciliation while providing excellent customer service.

Setup

To prepare for managing warranty claims, follow these steps:

  • Create a "Warranty Repairs" Item Type: This Item Type will help you categorize all warranty repair services.

  • Add a "Warranty Repairs" Manufacturer: Use this to classify warranty-related items under a specific manufacturer.

  • Set Up Warranty Repair Catalog Items: Include all necessary services and customer deductibles in the catalog.

  • Print SKU Barcodes: Generate a SKU sheet with barcodes for warranty services to streamline ticket processing.

  • Establish Billing Agents: Create agents to track receivables from warranty providers. Assign each agent a credit limit to ensure proper reconciliation.

Processing a Warranty Claim

To process a customer’s warranty claim, use the following workflow:

  1. Create a Repair Ticket: Enter the customer’s details and select the appropriate Billing Agent for the warranty provider.

  2. Add Warranty Service Items: Include relevant warranty services from the catalog on the ticket.

  3. Bundle Parts to Warranty Services: Associate required parts with their respective warranty service items.

  4. Record Deductible Payment: If applicable, collect and record the customer’s deductible payment.

  5. Advance the Ticket Through the Workflow: Move the ticket through your standard repair workflow until it reaches the Waiting for Payment status.

Reconciling Claims

Manual Payment Reconciliation

If the warranty provider pays manually:

  1. Record the payment from the provider against the ticket to close it out.

  2. Adjust balances if necessary for accurate accounting.

Enterprise Invoicing Reconciliation

For warranty providers using invoicing:

  1. Use the Invoicing Module to generate invoices for all tickets in the Waiting for Payment status.

  2. Add a Store Credit remittance to the invoice to mark it as paid in full.

  3. Review the Billing Agent’s credit history, which details payments made against invoices. The credit history for the Billing Agent itemizes the payments remitted against invoices.

  4. When a reimbursement check is received, record a Balance Adjustment Entry to account for the payment.

Reporting and Insights

Use the following reports to track and analyze warranty claims:

  • Sales Summary by Item Report: Filter by the "Warranty Repairs" item type for a detailed breakdown of all warranty services. The Sales Summary by Item Report, when filtered by Item Type of Warranty Repairs, shows an itemized breakdown of all warranty repairs.

  • Sales Details by Device Report: View warranty repairs itemized for each repair device. The Sales Details by Device Report shows an itemized breakdown of all warranty repairs associated with each repair device.

  • Productivity Repairs by Device Report: Track productivity metrics for warranty repairs linked to specific devices. The Productivity Repairs by Device Report shows an itemized breakdown of all warranty repairs associated with each repair device.
